title: MailerLite vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite and GetResponse. See pricing, features, and ratings to find the best email marketing tool for your needs.
h1: MailerLite vs GetResponse: A Detailed Comparison
intro: Both MailerLite and GetResponse are freemium email marketing platforms that serve different audience needs. MailerLite focuses on simplicity for small businesses, while GetResponse positions itself as a comprehensive online marketing platform with webinar and course creation capabilities. Understanding their differences in pricing, features, and target use cases will help you choose the right tool for your business.
choose_a_if: You're a small business or freelancer looking for an affordable, easy-to-use email marketing solution
You need strong e-commerce integrations and features like pop-ups and surveys
You want a larger free tier (1,000 subscribers) and lower entry-level pricing ($9/month)
choose_b_if: You plan to host webinars or create online courses as part of your marketing strategy
You need an all-in-one marketing platform beyond just email marketing
You're willing to pay more for advanced funnel and automation capabilities

faq.a: MailerLite is better for most small businesses and e-commerce owners due to its simplicity, affordability, and superior ratings (4.6 on G2 vs 4.2). However, GetResponse is better if you specifically need webinar hosting, online course creation, or advanced funnel-building tools.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or GetResponse?
faq.a: MailerLite is significantly cheaper, with a free plan supporting 1,000 subscribers and paid plans starting at $9/month. GetResponse offers only 500 free subscribers and starts at $19/month, making MailerLite the more budget-friendly choice for growing lists.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your contact lists and email templates. Most email marketing tools support standard CSV or contact import formats, making migration relatively straightforward. You may need to manually recreate some automations depending on their complexity.
